name: Permify Authorization API
what_it_does: Open-source Google Zanzibar-inspired authorization service providing relationship-based access control (ReBAC). Permify stores relationships (user is member of org, org owns document) and evaluates permissions via its gRPC and REST API. Supports RBAC, ABAC, and ReBAC patterns. Self-hostable with Permify Cloud managed option. Designed for multi-tenant SaaS authorization and agent permission management.
use_cases: Implement fine-grained agent authorization using relationship-based rules — 'agent X can access document Y if user Z granted it'
Build multi-tenant AI application permissions where agents can only access data belonging to their tenant
Check batch permissions for agent tool access — verify which tools an agent is allowed to invoke in a single API call
Implement hierarchical permissions for AI agent systems — parent agents can delegate subsets of their permissions to child agents
Store and query authorization state for AI applications — who owns what, who can access what, with audit trail
not_for: Simple RBAC with few roles — Permify's Zanzibar model adds complexity not needed for basic role-based systems
Policy-as-code enforcement with complex logic — OPA is better for conditional logic-based authorization
Teams not wanting to manage authorization state — Permify requires storing all relationships, not just rules
best_when: You're building a multi-tenant SaaS or agent system that needs Google Zanzibar-style relationship-based authorization with fine-grained, hierarchical permissions.
avoid_when: You have simple RBAC needs or need complex conditional authorization logic — simpler RBAC systems or OPA may be more appropriate.

auth.notes: API key authentication for service access. Keys generated per tenant in Permify. Token passed in Authorization header. gRPC uses metadata for auth. No scope granularity within a tenant.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is free (Apache 2.0). Permify Cloud has a free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: Permify Cloud Pro and Enterprise for managed hosting with SLA
pricing.notes: Open-source and self-hostable for free. Permify Cloud managed service for teams that don't want to manage infrastructure. Storage backend (PostgreSQL, memory) must be self-managed for self-hosted.

performance.rate_limits: No Permify-imposed limits for self-hosted; Cloud limits based on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns JSON errors with codes. gRPC returns typed status codes. Check permission API returns boolean result clearly. Relationship write errors include entity and relation context. Documentation is good with schema examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Permission checks are fully idempotent. Relationship writes are idempotent — writing the same tuple twice is a no-op. Deletes are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Schema (entity types, relations, permissions) must be defined before writing relationships — schema changes require careful migration
Relationship consistency model — check if your use case requires snapshot consistency or eventual consistency
Bulk relationship writes should be batched — individual writes for large datasets are slow; use the batch write API
Permission check latency scales with relationship graph depth — deeply nested hierarchies can be slow to evaluate
Tenant isolation is at the application level — Permify supports multi-tenancy via tenant ID but doesn't enforce tenant isolation at storage level in all backends
Schema evolution requires careful versioning — changing relation definitions may require relationship data migration
Permify is early-stage — breaking changes between versions are possible; pin version and test upgrades carefully

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source — auditable. Self-hosted option for full data control. SOC2 for Cloud. gRPC TLS enforced. Authorization data stored in your database. Strong security-first design philosophy.
